rationale: The app is an accessibility utility for non-verbal users. It requires high contrast, calming tones, large touch targets, and a completely unobstructed camera view. The core screen uses a dark/translucent overlay theme to guarantee text legibility over any real-world background.

camera_overlay_panel.style: Glassmorphism over camera: absolute positioning, bottom-0, w-full, pb-safe-area, pt-6, px-4. Background using Expo BlurView with 'dark' tint or rgba(0,0,0,0.55).
camera_overlay_panel.emotion_badge: Floating badge, top-safe-area left-4. Uses Reanimated for smooth color transitions based on emotion state. Contains icon + text + confidence bar (width driven by shared value).
camera_overlay_panel.action_buttons: Circular FABs (56x56 minimum) with solid background (white or primary) to ensure visibility over camera. Ambient shadow applied for depth.

sentence_builder.style: Horizontal scroll view inside the bottom glass panel. Tap words to read aloud, Clear button on the right edge.

Onboarding.layout: Vertical scroll, large imagery at top, clear typography. 'Next'/'Get Started' button sticky at bottom.
elements: Hero image showing diverse sign language users.
Feature highlights (Real-time translation, Emotion tracking, TTS).
Large 'Get Started' CTA (min 56px height, rounded-full, primary color).

Camera_Home.layout: Full screen Camera (Expo Camera). Absolute positioned UI overlays.
elements: Top-Right: Settings Icon & Flip Camera Icon (translucent dark circles, white icons).
Top-Left: Emotion Badge (Dynamic color, confidence progress bar).
Bottom Panel: Translucent Expo BlurView container.
Bottom Panel -> Text output area (large white text, clear contrast).
Bottom Panel -> Controls: TTS Speak Button, Clear Button, Capture/Analyze Button (Prominent center FAB).
alerts: Caregiver Distress Alert: Slides down from top safe-area (z-index highest) with Red-Orange background if Distress+Help is detected.

History.layout: Standard List view on Light Background.
elements: List items: Timestamp, Detected Text, Emotion Icon. Clean 1px divider lines (#E5E5EA).

Settings.layout: Grouped list view on Light Background.

animations.details: Use useSharedValue and withTiming for confidence bar widths and emotion badge background colors. Use entering/exiting layout animations (FadeInDown/FadeOutUp) for text chunks appearing in the sentence builder.

icons.usage: Use clear, recognizable icons: 'volume-high' for TTS, 'camera-reverse' for flip, 'close-circle' for clear, 'alert-circle' for distress.

onboarding_sign_language.description: Person demonstrating ASL in a black jacket

accessibility_testing.contrast_notes: All text over the camera MUST use a semi-transparent dark backing layer (or strict text shadow) to guarantee readability regardless of camera feed lighting (APCA contrast requirement).

instructions_to_main_agent: Use expo-camera for the main view.
Implement the UI using React Native standard components (View, Text, TouchableOpacity/Pressable) styled with either Tailwind (via NativeWind) or StyleSheet.
Do NOT use web components like Shadcn, HTML tags, or browser-specific CSS.
Use Expo BlurView for glassmorphism panels over the camera.
Implement smooth animations for the confidence bar using react-native-reanimated.
Ensure all touch targets (buttons, icons) have a minimum dimension of 56x56 pixels for motor-accessibility.
Attach testID attributes to all interactive elements matching the accessibility_testing data_testids.
